post.description:
Create or update scenario via public API

- If 'id' is not provided or null: Creates a new scenario
- If 'id' is provided: Updates the existing scenario with that ID

Only explicitly provided fields will be updated during update operations.
All monetization fields (pricing, Stripe integration) are supported.

Org Context:
- Supports X-TT-ORG header for organization-scoped scenarios
- If valid org_id is provided, scenario will be created in org context

get.description:
List all scenarios through the public API with org context support.

Returns:
- Scenarios created by the user (with org filtering)
- Scenarios where the user has permissions (with org filtering)
- Scenarios where the org has permissions (if in org context)
- When is_featured=true: globally featured public scenarios (org ignored)

Filters:
- search: case-insensitive substring match on name + description
- meta_<key>=value (exact match on user_metadata)
- meta_$gt_<key>=value, meta_$gte_<key>=value, meta_$lt_<key>=value, meta_$lte_<key>=value

post.description:
Create a short-lived access token for a scenario (1-24 hour duration) to enable
embedding private scenarios in iframes.

Access Requirements:
- Must be creator OR have READ+ permission on the scenario.
- Org context validated automatically via Permission.has_access.

Email Parameter Behavior:
- No email: SAT for anonymous user (anyone can do this).
- ORG context + email: Must be format `*@<ORG-ID>.toughtongueai.com` (dormant user).
- SaaS context + email: Reseller-only, creates real TTAI user.

Dormant Users (ORG only):
- Cannot login to ToughTongue.
- Enable memory features tied to org-scoped identifier
- Email format: `random-string@<ORG-ID>.toughtongueai.com`
Self-billed SAT — PAT holder is the runner. Public scenarios bypass ACL; others require creator/READ.

post.description:
Create a Stripe checkout session for a scenario or collection purchase through the API.
NOTE: Endpoint URL is a misnomer. It's supposed to be `checkout-session` (singular).

This endpoint:
1. Resolves or creates the user based on email
2. Validates that the product exists and is available for purchase
3. Creates a Stripe checkout session
4. Returns the checkout URL that the API user can redirect to

The API token owner must have access rights to the product and must be a reseller.

get.description:
List sessions with evaluation and improvement results.

Response includes:
- evaluation_results: Full evaluation (score, report_card, strengths, weaknesses)
- improvement_results: Improvement areas, action items, resources

Filters:
- scenario_id, user_email, is_org, page, limit

Date filters (convenience):
- from_date / to_date: shorthand for updated_at range.
  Accepts YYYY-MM-DD or full ISO. to_date bumps to end-of-day automatically.

Date filters (advanced — $op_<field> convention, same as metadata):
- $gte_updated_at, $gt_updated_at, $lte_updated_at, $lt_updated_at
- $gte_created_at, $gt_created_at, $lte_created_at, $lt_created_at
- ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD or full ISO timestamp

Both styles can be used together; advanced filters take precedence on overlap.

Metadata filters:
- meta_<key>=value (exact match on user_metadata)
- meta_$gt_<key>=value, meta_$gte_<key>=value, meta_$lt_<key>=value, meta_$lte_<key>=value

Deprecated (removal: 2026-09-30):
- duration_minutes → use: duration / 60
- evaluation_score → use: evaluation_results.final_score
- evaluation_note → use: evaluation_results.overall_score
- report_card → use: evaluation_results.report_card
- report_card_topics → iterate evaluation_results.report_card

get.description:
Get unified analytics data in a single API call.

Personal view (is_org_wide=false):
- Returns scenario stats only

Org view (is_org_wide=true):
- Returns scenario stats (org-wide)
- Returns org dashboard (time series, usage summary, wallet balance)
- Returns member usage breakdown
- Requires EDIT or OWNER role in the organization

Query params:
- is_org_wide: bool (default: false) - Toggle between personal and org-wide view
- start_date: datetime (optional) - Filter analytics from this date (default: 90 days ago)
- end_date: datetime (optional) - Filter analytics until this date (default: today)

post.description:
Initiate a single outbound SIP call.

Requires:
- User must own or have VIEW permission on the scenario
- Phone number must be in E.164 format (e.g., +14155551234)

If scheduled_ts is provided, the call will be scheduled for that time.
Otherwise, the call is initiated immediately.

Create a batch of SIP calls.

Requires:
- User must own or have VIEW permission on the scenario
- All phone numbers must be in E.164 format

Batch calls are processed asynchronously via a cron job (every 5 minutes).
Max concurrency is determined by user's SIP trunk configuration.

Create a completed session from transcript or recording URL.

Requires VIEW access to scenario. Analysis runs in background.

Run post-session processing (analysis and/or extraction) in the background.

Pass flags to control which jobs run. Calling this always (re-)runs the requested jobs —
results overwrite any existing. Fetch them later via `GET /sessions/{id}`.

This is the unified successor to v1's `/sessions/analyze` (which will be deprecated)
and replaces the v1 `/sessions/extract` endpoint.
Generate scenario content from a name or context document.

Create a Browserbase context + session for authenticated browser demos.

Checks that the scenario exists and has the browser tool enabled.
Returns an embed_url the caller opens once to log in; auth state persists
for all future demo sessions.

EvalReportEntry.title: EvalReportEntry
EvalReportEntry.description:
A container class to represent evaluation report for a single topic / criterion / rubrik entry.
It contains the following fields:
- topic: str
- score: Optional[float]
- score_str: Optional[str]
- note: Optional[str]

LKScenario.title: LKScenario
LKScenario.description:
Scenario-derived config shipped to the LiveKit agent worker in room metadata.

LLM / voice:
    ``llm_provider + llm_model`` pick the engine; ``voice`` is the TTS voice name
    (provider-specific string). ``cascade_llm_provider`` is only meaningful when
    ``llm_provider == "cascade"``.

Tools — three distinct buckets:
    ``tools``         — scenario-declared; filtered per sub-agent via ``SubAgent.tool_ids``.
    ``system_tools`` — injected at connect time (e.g. MeetingTool for rtChat); bypass
                        ``tool_ids`` and go to every agent unconditionally.
    ``server_tools``  — Python-side tools (end_session, cold_transfer, knowledge_base_search)
                        keyed by tool-id with a settings dict; populated only for SIP sessions
                        by ``SIPService.resolve_server_tools()``.

Super-agent:
    ``stages`` drives the SuperAgent/FlowAgent multi-stage graph; absent means a
    single TTAgent handles everything. ``voice_language`` is the BCP-47 hint passed
    to the STT engine (distinct from ``transcription_language`` which is per-turn).

Behaviour & features:
    ``strategy`` carries ``skip_auto_start`` and other session modifiers.
    ``recording`` and ``avatar_config`` are feature sub-configs with their own defaults.

MeetingBot.title: MeetingBot
MeetingBot.description:
A scheduled call or meeting session driven by a scenario (agent).

In code this is typically just called `bot` — the "Meeting" prefix is a
historical artefact from when only Recall meeting-bots existed.

Represents 1:1 with either:
- a **Recall bot** (`call_type="meeting_bot"`) — joins a VC room
- a **SIP bot** (`call_type="sip_call"` or `"sip_inbound"`) — phone call via LiveKit SIP

- **Two call types** share this collection:
  `meeting_bot` (Recall VC) and `sip_call` (LiveKit SIP phone call).
  SIP-specific fields are null for meeting bots and vice-versa.
- **Ownership:** `user_id` + optional `org`, scoped to one `scenario_id`.
- **Scheduling:** `calendar_auto` | `scheduled_manual` | `instant_manual`.
- **Identifiers:** `bot.id` is our MongoDB ObjectId (NOT Recall's bot ID).
  `recall_bot_id` and `recall_calendar_event_id` are Recall.ai UUIDs.
  The latter is the upsert identity key for calendar_auto bots.
- **Schema layout:** queried fields stay flat (indexed); inspection-only
  fields nest into sub-docs (`recall_meta`, `calendar_event_meta`).
- **Deprecated flat fields:** `bot_name`, `event_name` — retained for
  backward compat. Canonical source is `recall_meta`. Use accessors
  `get_bot_name()` / `get_event_name()`.
- **Logs:** append-only list capped at `LOG_LIMIT` via `$push/$slice`.

S3Resource.title: S3Resource
S3Resource.description:
Typed, self-describing pointer to an S3 object.

Args:
    bucket:    Target S3 bucket (must be one of the declared S3Bucket literals).
    key:       Path fragment **after** the namespace prefix.
               Format: `{feature}/[{YYYY-MM-DD}/]{filename}`
               Do NOT include `users/{uid}/` or `public/` — those are prepended
               by `resolve_path()`.
    is_public: If True, resolves to `public/{key}` and `user_id` is ignored.
               If False, resolves to `users/{user_id}/{key}` (user_id required).
    user_id:   MongoDB ObjectId string of the owning user.
               Required when `is_public=False`; ignored when `is_public=True`.
    dated:     If True, `resolve_path()` injects today's date (YYYY-MM-DD) as a
               sub-directory immediately before the filename segment.
               Only meaningful at write time — stored keys already contain the
               date segment so dated=False at read time is fine.

SessionTimelineEvent.title: SessionTimelineEvent
SessionTimelineEvent.description:
Polymorphic event in the meeting timeline (:timeline Redis LIST).

type discriminates behaviour:
  chat       — text sent by a participant or the bot (relayed in real-time)
  join       — participant entered the meeting (relayed in real-time)
  leave      — participant left the meeting (relayed in real-time)
  bot_status — bot lifecycle change, e.g. joining/recording/ended (timeline only)
  log        — operational metadata pushed by the backend at key moments,
               e.g. session context at connect, participant snapshots after
               join/leave. Read by the agent via get_timeline; never relayed.

Fields not relevant to a given type are left at their defaults
(text="" for presence events; participant_id=None for chat/log/bot_status).

Not to be confused with `SessionEvent` (agent telemetry events stored in
agentStore.events / Session.metadata.events).

SubAgent.title: SubAgent
SubAgent.description:
A sub-agent of the super-agent.

``ai_model`` controls how this sub-agent relates to the supervisor's
voice pipeline:

- **inherit** (``ai_model=None``, default): sub-agent speaks using
  the supervisor's session (LLM + STT + TTS).
- **standby** (``ai_model={provider:"standby", model:"standby"}``):
  sub-agent is silent — ``tts_node`` drains text, produces no audio.
  Only tool calls and handoffs.
- **informational override** (any other ``{provider, model}``):
  value is shown in the LLM prompt (``ai_str()``) but the sub-agent
  still shares the supervisor's session.  Reserved for future
  per-flow model switching.

SubAgentModel.title: SubAgentModel
SubAgentModel.description:
Per-flow (Flow = Sub-Agent) model declaration for a sub-agent.

Uses plain strings (not enums) for flexibility — Claude Box YAMLs can
set arbitrary provider/model combos without backend enum changes.

Functional modes today:
    - ``standby``  (provider="standby", model="standby")
      Activates silent mode on the FlowAgent: ``tts_node`` is muted,
      prompt switches to tool-only instructions.
    - Any other value is **informational** — shown in the LLM-visible
      ``ai_str()`` but the sub-agent still shares the supervisor's
      session (LLM, STT, TTS).  True per-flow model switching is not
      yet implemented.

Compared to the top-level ``AIModelConfig`` (scenario → supervisor):
    AIModelConfig adds cascade-specific fields (tts_provider,
    tts_voice_id, llm_provider, llm_model, stt_provider) that are
    not needed here because sub-agents share the supervisor's session.
    If per-flow model switching is implemented in the future, this
    schema would need to be extended for cascade sub-agents.
